summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is a linear pair?

Linear Pair A Linear Pair is a pair of adjacent angles whose non-common sides form a straight Line. The other options — 'Two vertical angles', 'Two angles that are both acute', and 'Angles on opposite sides of a transversal' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

If one angle of a linear pair measures 65°, what is the other?

Linear Pair A Linear Pair is a pair of adjacent angles whose non-common sides form a straight Line. The other options — '25°', '65°', and '90°' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
